title: Chaenorhinum flexuosum
taxonomy: Chaenorhinum flexuosum is classified within the plant kingdom as a member of the family Scrophulariaceae. This family is a part of the order Lamiales. At the class level, it belongs to the Magnoliopsida. Further classification places it within the subclass Asteridae and the clade Angiosperms. Ultimately, it is categorized as a dicotyledonous plant under the kingdom Plantae.

The plant's uses in medicine are primarily based on anecdotal evidence, and more scientific investigation is necessary.

The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it potentially useful for soothing skin irritations and wounds. Its leaves and stems have been used in herbal remedies to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and ulcers. intro: Chaenorhinum flexuosum, commonly known as the sticky Chaenorhinum, is a flowering plant in the family Scrophulariaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to provide relief from various health issues, such as pain and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Chaenorhinum flexuosum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Chaenorhinum flexuosum is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to treat skin conditions and wounds.
